title: Connarus trifoliatus
intro: Connarus trifoliatus, also known as the three-leaved Connarus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its purported health benefits. Bioactive constituents of Connarus trifoliatus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Connarus trifoliatus include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, coughs, and colds.
taxonomy: Connarus trifoliatus is a species in the family Connaraceae. The Connaraceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the staff trees or staff vines.
